name: Alex Dathskovsky
intro: Alex Dathskovsky has over 17 years of software development experience, working on systems, low-level generic tools and high-level applications. He has held technical roles at Elbit, Rafael, Axxana, and Abbott Israel, and currently serves as a group manager and technical leader at Speedata.io — a startup focused on big data and analytics solutions. His present role involves developing CPU/APU systems utilizing C++20 and advanced template metaprogramming, along with LLVM development. Alex specializes in C++ and template metaprogramming, and actively teaches courses on modern C++ features, advocating for industry adoption of current language standards.
bio: Group Manager and Technical Manager at Speedata.io with 17+ years of software development experience across embedded systems, big data, and high-performance computing. Previous roles include Technical Leader at Axxana, Software Manager at Abbott Israel, Senior SW Developer at Rafael, and integration developer at Elbit. Expert in C++20, massive template metaprogramming, and LLVM development. Also teaches a modern C++ features course.

name: Ankur M Satle
intro: Ankur Satle is an Architect at EXFO specializing in telecom products. With two decades of experience, he has worked extensively on high-performance 3G/4G/5G/IMS/VoIP products, Network Monitoring, and OSS/BSS systems, and is currently developing a Smart Data Platform for 5G/4G/3G Service Assurance. He advocates for building reusable libraries and emphasizes creating software that is easy-to-use-correctly, correct-by-construction, and minimalistic. An active CppIndia organizer, presenter, and maintainer who has presented at CppCon 2020–2022. Beyond programming, he pursues natural language learning, outdoor activities, drawing, football, and discussions on cosmology.
bio: Architect at EXFO working on telecom products (3G/4G/5G/IMS/VoIP, Network Monitoring, OSS/BSS) with two decades of experience. B.Tech in Electronics and Telecom with a Post-graduate Diploma in Software Technology. Passionate about code that is easy-to-use-correctly, correct-by-construction, and minimalistic. Has presented at CppCon 2020, 2021, and 2022. Active CppIndia organiser, presenter, and maintainer.

name: John Stratton
topic: Software Design and Performance in C++
intro: John Stratton serves as CTO of MulticoreWare and Associate Professor of Computer Science at Whitman College, with a Ph.D. in Electrical and Computer Engineering from the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign. His professional focus centers on software that is both well-designed and high-performing. He designed MxPA — the first licensable implementation of OpenCL — which formed the foundation for numerous accelerator companies' OpenCL implementations. His current teaching emphasizes software performance optimization and simulation methods while maintaining good software design practices.
bio: CTO of MulticoreWare (since 2010) and Associate Professor of Computer Science at Whitman College. Ph.D. in Electrical and Computer Engineering from the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign (2013). Designed and developed MxPA — the first licensable OpenCL implementation. Published research on Halide and OpenCL compilers for DSPs, and advanced scientific computing through stochastic simulation and image analysis.

name: Jonathan Müller
topic: An (In-)Complete Guide to C++ Object Lifetimes
intro: Jonathan Müller is a Software Engineer at think-cell where he maintains the core libraries. He has authored several popular C++ open-source projects: type_safe (safety utilities), foonathan/memory (memory allocator library), lexy (parser library), and lauf (bytecode interpreter). More recently, his focus has shifted to programming languages and compilers. He serves on the C++ standardization committee as assistant chair for std::ranges, and blogs at foonathan.net.
bio: Software Engineer at think-cell maintaining core libraries. Creator of several popular open-source C++ projects: type_safe, foonathan/memory (memory allocator library), lexy (parser library), and lauf (bytecode interpreter). Blogs at foonathan.net. C++ standardization committee member — assistant chair for std::ranges.

name: Mahendra Bhagwan Garodi
intro: Mahendra Bhagwan Garodi has over 10 years of experience developing virtual platforms in the semiconductor industry. He has modeled functional and microarchitectural details of CPUs, memories, and peripheral components in SoCs. His speaking topics span performance evaluation, SIMD architecture, undefined behaviour, and contemporary C++ features. An active CppIndia community member who delivers presentations across multiple technical domains, and also teaches C, C++, Python, Data Structures, and Mathematics.
bio: 10+ years developing virtual platforms in the semiconductor industry, focusing on functional and microarchitectural modelling of CPUs, memories, and peripheral components in SoCs. Active CppIndia community member and regular presenter — covering SIMD, performance evaluation, undefined behaviour, and modern C++ features. Also teaches C, C++, Python, Data Structures, and Mathematics.

name: Mike Shah
topic: Getting Started with Modern C++ — A Tour of Features
intro: Mike Shah is an Associate Teaching Professor at Northeastern University, Khoury College of Computer Sciences. His primary teaching interests are computer systems, computer graphics, and software engineering. His research interests are related to performance engineering (dynamic analysis), software visualization, and computer graphics. Beyond academia, he works occasionally as a 3D Senior Graphics Engineer in C++ and creates educational programming content on YouTube.
bio: Associate Teaching Professor at Northeastern University, Khoury College of Computer Sciences. Teaching interests: computer systems, graphics, and software engineering. Research interests: performance engineering (dynamic analysis), software visualization, and computer graphics. Occasional consulting work as a 3D Senior Graphics Engineer in C++. Runs a popular C++ YouTube channel.

name: Pavithra Ashok Kumar
intro: Pavithra Ashok Kumar has around 10 years of experience working across different languages including C++, MATLAB, OCaml, and Java. She loves abstractions and talking to the compiler.
bio: Software engineer with ~10 years of experience across C++, MATLAB, OCaml, and Java. Loves abstractions and talking to the compiler. Passionate about systematic debugging approaches and writing correct-by-construction software.

name: Šimon Tóth
intro: Šimon Tóth is an ex-SWE and ex-Researcher sharing 20 years worth of industry experience with the world. He is the author of the Daily bit(e) of C++ series, A Complete Guide to Standard C++ Algorithms book, and Surviving the C++ Coding Interview.
bio: Ex-software engineer and researcher sharing 20 years of industry experience with the world. Author of the 'Daily bit(e) of C++' series, 'A Complete Guide to Standard C++ Algorithms' book, and 'Surviving the C++ Coding Interview'. Focused on making modern C++ features accessible to all developers.

name: Bryce Adelstein Lelbach
intro: Bryce Adelstein Lelbach is the HPC Programming Models Architect at NVIDIA, leading language standardization initiatives and technical direction for the company's HPC compilers and libraries. He chairs INCITS/PL22 (the US standards committee for programming languages), the Standard C++ Library Evolution group, and serves as program chair for C++Now and CppCon conferences. With over a decade of experience in programming language and software library development, his C++ Committee contributions span concurrency primitives, parallel algorithms, executors, and multidimensional arrays. He was among the initial developers of the HPX parallel runtime system.
bio: HPC Programming Models Architect at NVIDIA. Leads programming language standardization and drives the technical roadmap for NVIDIA's HPC compilers and libraries. Chairs INCITS/PL22 (the US standards committee for programming languages) and the Standard C++ Library Evolution working group. Editor for INCITS Inclusive Terminology Guidelines. Program chair for C++Now and CppCon conferences. C++ Committee contributor for concurrency primitives, parallel algorithms, executors, and multidimensional arrays. Initial developer of the HPX parallel runtime system.

name: Mike Shah
intro: Mike Shah is an Associate Teaching Professor at Northeastern University's Khoury College of Computer Sciences. His academic focus encompasses computer systems, graphics, and software engineering instruction. His research interests include performance engineering through dynamic analysis, software visualization, and computer graphics. Beyond academia, he has pursued consulting roles as a senior 3D graphics engineer specializing in C++ development, and creates educational programming content on YouTube.
bio: Associate Teaching Professor at Northeastern University, Khoury College of Computer Sciences. Research focuses on performance engineering (dynamic analysis), software visualization, and computer graphics. Occasional consulting as a 3D Senior Graphics Engineer in C++. Popular C++ educator on YouTube.

name: Kiran Kannappan
intro: Kiran Kannappan has been working in the Chip Design Industry for about 25 years, after completing his Masters at the Indian Institute of Science, with various Fortune 500 companies at various geographic locations. Since his return to India close to a decade ago, he has been involved in building AI Applications and enhancing performance for HPC applications using Intel's oneAPI toolkit.
bio: 25 years of experience in the Chip Design Industry. Masters from the Indian Institute of Science. Since returning to India approximately a decade ago, focused on building AI Applications and enhancing performance for HPC applications using Intel's oneAPI toolkit.

name: Jonathan Müller
topic: C++ Features You Might Not Know
intro: Jonathan Müller is a library developer at think-cell. In his spare time, he works on various C++ open-source libraries for memory allocation, cache-friendly containers, and parsing. He maintains a technical blog at foonathan.net and actively participates in the C++ standardization committee.
bio: Library developer at think-cell. Creator of open-source C++ projects covering memory allocation, cache-friendly containers, and parsing. Blogs at foonathan.net. Active C++ standardization committee member.

name: Venkata Naga Ravikiran Bulusu
intro: Venkata Naga Ravikiran Bulusu is a C/C++ Embedded Developer with 6 years of experience in Embedded Systems. Currently at VITES GmbH in Munich, Germany, developing C/C++17 software for Embedded Linux systems for ground station terminals communicating with commercial satellites. He holds a Masters in Microsystems from Hochschule Bremen and is active in the CppCon and ISO C++ standards community.
bio: C/C++ Embedded Developer with 6 years of experience in Embedded Systems. Currently at VITES GmbH, Munich, Germany, developing C/C++17 software for Embedded Linux systems for ground station terminals communicating with commercial satellites. Masters in Microsystems from Hochschule Bremen, Germany. Active in the CppCon and ISO C++ standards community.

name: Ankur M Satle
intro: Ankur Satle is an Architect at EXFO specializing in Telecom products. With two decades of experience, he has worked extensively on high-performance 3G/4G/5G/IMS/VoIP products, Network Monitoring, and OSS/BSS systems. He advocates for creating reusable libraries and leveraging open-source or InnerSource strategies, with design principles that emphasize implementations that are easy-to-use-correctly, correct-by-construction, and minimalistic. An active CppIndia organizer, presenter, and maintainer who has presented at CppCon 2020, 2021, and 2022.
bio: Architect at EXFO working on 5G/4G/3G Service Assurance and Network Monitoring with two decades of experience. B.Tech in Electronics and Telecom; PG Diploma in Software Technology. Active CppIndia organiser, presenter, and maintainer. Personal interests include natural languages (Indian + French), outdoor activities, drawing, cosmology, football, and thriller/mystery films.

name: Andreas Fertig
intro: Andreas Fertig is the CEO of Unique Code GmbH and an experienced trainer and lecturer for C++ (standards 11 to 20), actively participating in the C++ standards committee. He is the creator of C++ Insights — an internationally recognized tool that enables users to look behind the scenes of C++. Prior to his current role, he spent a decade at Philips Medizin Systeme GmbH as a C++ software developer and architect specializing in embedded systems. He publishes specialist articles in iX magazine and has authored multiple C++ textbooks.
bio: CEO of Unique Code GmbH. Trainer and lecturer for C++ (standards 11 to 20) and participant in C++ standardization. Creator of 'C++ Insights' — an internationally recognized tool that enables users to look behind the scenes of C++. Previously spent a decade at Philips Medical Systems as a C++ developer in embedded architecture. Publishes in iX magazine and has authored multiple C++ textbooks.

name: Kushashwa Ravi Shrimali
topic: Using C++ for Your Artificial Intelligence Solution
intro: Kushashwa Ravi Shrimali is a CSE graduate from IIIT NR, India (Gold Medalist, Transformational Leadership, class of 2020), currently a Backend Engineer at Abnormal Security. He has been a lead contributor to Lightning Flash, Research Engineer at Lightning AI, SDE at Quansight working on PyTorch with Meta, SDE intern at NVIDIA's PyTorch Dev Team, AI Engineer at CareAI, and R&D intern at ROSE Labs, NTU Singapore. He has made significant open-source contributions to PyTorch and OpenCV with a focus on C++ integration for deep learning.
bio: CSE Graduate ('20) from IIIT NR, India (Gold Medalist, Transformational Leadership). Currently Backend Engineer at Abnormal Security. Past: Lead contributor to Lightning Flash; contributed to PyTorch and OpenCV with C++; Research Engineer at Lightning AI; SDE at Quansight (PyTorch/Meta); SDE intern at NVIDIA (Santa Clara); AI Engineer at CareAI; R&D Intern at ROSE Labs NTU Singapore.

name: Selvakumar Jawahar
topic: Why 5G is Fast? and C++ in 5G Open Source
intro: Selvakumar Jawahar is a Senior Engineer at Juniper Networks with over 20 years of experience in developing complex, high-quality software and embedded systems. He specializes in architecting next-generation Quality Assurance solutions for Cloud, Datacenter, and 5G networks. Among all the languages he works with, he particularly favors C++.
bio: Senior Engineer at Juniper Networks with 20+ years of experience building complex, high-quality software and embedded systems. Specializes in architecting next-generation QA solutions for cloud, datacenter, and 5G networks.

name: Prince Gupta
bio: Software engineer interested in compiler optimizations and low-level performance tuning in C++. Presented a lightning talk at CppIndiaCon 2023 on writing code that allows the compiler to automatically vectorize loops using SIMD instructions.

name: Emmanuel Danso Nyarko
topic: Understanding Dynamic Allocators for Optimization in Containers
bio: Software engineer focused on memory management and performance optimization in C++. Delivered a lightning talk at CppIndiaCon 2023 on custom allocators and how they can dramatically improve container performance in real-world applications.

name: Mahendra Garodi
bio: Software engineer in the semiconductor industry with 10+ years of experience modelling SoCs. Active CppIndia community member who has delivered talks and lightning talks at multiple CppIndiaCon editions.

name: Inbal Levi
topic: Customization Methods: Connecting User And Library Code
intro: Inbal Levi is an embedded software engineer specializing in high-performance systems. She holds several leadership positions within the C++ community: director at the ISO C++ Foundation, co-chair of Library Evolution, and chair of SG9 (the Ranges group) within the C++ Standards Committee. She also serves as chair of the ISO C++ Israeli National Body and co-organizes the CoreCpp conference and user group.
bio: Embedded software engineer with a passion for high performance. Director at the ISO C++ Foundation. Co-chair of Library Evolution and Chair of SG9 (Ranges group) of the C++ Standards Committee. Chair of the ISO C++ Israeli National Body. Organizer of the CoreCpp conference and user group.

name: Hemil Ruparel
topic: Default Generated Comparison for Your Data Types
bio: Independent Contractor and Freelancer who helps people build apps. C++ was his first programming language. Interests span OS, distributed computing, compilers/interpreters, programming language design, and hardware. Was in 3rd year B.Tech Computer Engineering at Bharati Vidyapeeth, Pune at the time of speaking. Also a CppIndia community moderator.

name: Selvakumar Jawahar
intro: Selvakumar Jawahar is a Senior Software Engineer at Juniper Networks with over 19 years of professional experience. He specializes in designing high-performance embedded systems, engineering large-scale enterprise software, and building real-time web applications. He deeply cares about building exceptional quality and high-performance software systems, and particularly favors C++ among all the languages he works with.
bio: Senior Software Engineer at Juniper Networks with 19+ years of experience building high-performance embedded systems, large-scale enterprise software, and real-time web applications.

name: Dr. Greg Law
topic: Getting the Most Out of GDB
intro: Dr. Greg Law is the founder of Undo — a time travel debugging company for Linux software development. He brings over two decades of experience in the software industry, having worked in development and management roles at Acorn, NexWave, and Solarflare. Motivated by the challenges developers face when debugging complex software, he established Undo from his garden shed in 2012. The company has since become a recognized leader in time-travel debugging.
bio: Founder of Undo — the time travel debugging company for Linux software development. 20+ years in the software industry with previous roles in development and management at Acorn (the British computer firm), NexWave, and Solarflare. Founded Undo from his garden shed in 2012.

name: Ankur M Satle
intro: Ankur Satle is an Architect at EXFO with 19+ years of experience in high-performance 3G/4G/5G/IMS/VoIP products, Network Monitoring, and OSS/BSS systems. His design philosophy emphasizes creating software that is easy-to-use-correctly, correct-by-construction, and minimalistic. He holds a B.Tech. in Electronics and Telecom plus a postgraduate diploma in Software Technology. Active CppIndia organizer and presenter with talks at CppCon 2020 and 2021.
bio: Architect at EXFO with 19+ years of experience. Active CppIndia organiser, presenter, and maintainer. Has also presented at CppCon 2020 and 2021.

name: Bjarne Stroustrup
intro: Bjarne Stroustrup is the creator of C++, having initiated its development in 1979 under the original name 'C with Classes.' He remains deeply involved with the language, serving on the ISO C++ standards committee. His professional accomplishments include the Draper Award and the Faraday Medal. Currently, he holds the position of Technical Fellow and Managing Director within Morgan Stanley's technology division in New York City, while also serving as a Visiting Professor of Computer Science at Columbia University.
bio: Creator of C++ (began development in 1979). Invented C++, wrote its early definitions, and produced its first implementation. Designed all major language facilities and managed extension proposals through the C++ standards committee. Awards: Draper Award, Faraday Medal. Current roles: Technical Fellow and Managing Director at Morgan Stanley (NYC); Visiting Professor in Computer Science at Columbia University.

name: Jon Kalb
topic: C++ Today: The Beast is Back
intro: Jon Kalb is using his decades of software engineering experience and knowledge about C++ to make other people better software engineers. He trains experienced programmers, presents at technical conferences, and helps run user groups. He demonstrates a strong commitment to code quality and modern C++ development, with particular interest in leveraging contemporary hardware capabilities through standard, portable C++.
bio: Decades of software engineering experience. Uses his knowledge to make other people better software engineers — conducts training for experienced developers, presents at technical conferences, and helps manage user groups. Focused on quality code and modern C++ practices.

name: Bryce Adelstein Lelbach
topic: What Belongs in the C++ Standard Library
intro: Bryce Adelstein Lelbach is the HPC Programming Models Architect at NVIDIA, where he leads programming language standardization initiatives and directs the technical strategy for NVIDIA's HPC compilers and libraries. With over a decade of experience in programming language and software library development, he chairs INCITS/PL22, the Standard C++ Library Evolution group, and serves as program chair for C++Now and CppCon. His C++ Committee contributions span concurrency primitives, parallel algorithms, executors, and multidimensional arrays. He was among the initial developers of the HPX parallel runtime system.
bio: HPC Programming Models Architect at NVIDIA. Leads programming language standardization and drives the technical roadmap for NVIDIA's HPC compilers and libraries. Chairs INCITS/PL22 and the Standard C++ Library Evolution working group. Program chair for C++Now and CppCon. Initial developer of the HPX parallel runtime system.

name: Harishankar Singh
topic: C++20 Ranges: Code Expressively with Ranges
bio: 15 years in the software industry across domains including Avionics, Media, AI, Speech Recognition, and ML. Independent Trainer and Mentor for C++ since April 2020, focused on training professionals and students in sustainable, clean code practices.

name: Ankur M Satle
topic: C++20 Concepts — The Base Classes of Generic Programming?
intro: Ankur Satle is an Architect at EXFO with 18+ years of experience in VoIP, 3G/4G/IMS protocols, Network Monitoring, and OSS/BSS systems. He champions the development of reusable libraries and leveraging open-source solutions, with a design philosophy emphasizing software that is easy-to-use-correctly, correct-by-construction, and minimalistic. He holds a B.Tech. in Electronics and Telecom plus a postgraduate diploma in Software Technology. Actively engaged with CppIndia as an organizer, presenter, and maintainer.
bio: Architect at EXFO with 18+ years of experience in VoIP, 3G/4G/IMS protocols, Network Monitoring, and OSS/BSS. Active CppIndia founder and presenter who has spoken at all four editions of CppIndiaCon.

name: Saurabh Joshi
topic: Contract Programming: What, Why, and How?
intro: Saurabh Joshi is an Assistant Professor in the Computer Science and Engineering department at IIT Hyderabad. His academic journey includes positions at IIT Guwahati, University of Oxford, Microsoft Research, and IBM India Research Lab. He earned his Master's degree from IIT Bombay and his doctorate from IIT Kanpur. His research focuses on Formal Verification, Static Program Analysis, and Constraint Programming. He co-created Pinaka (a symbolic execution engine recognized at SVCOMP 2019–2021) and contributed to OpenWBO and OpenWBO-Inc (award-winning MaxSAT solvers in MaxSAT Evaluations 2014–2019).
bio: Assistant Professor in the CSE Department at IIT Hyderabad. Previous positions include IIT Guwahati, University of Oxford, Microsoft Research, and IBM India Research Lab. Masters from IIT Bombay; Doctorate from IIT Kanpur. Research areas: Formal Verification, Static Program Analysis, and Constraint Programming. Creator of Pinaka (symbolic execution engine — awarded at SVCOMP 2019, 2020, 2021) and OpenWBO/OpenWBO-Inc (MaxSAT solvers, winners in MaxSAT Evaluations 2014–2019).
